reviews.review_description:
I wanted to like this game... I really did. It's just very inaccurate historically. I might of let that pass if the graphics were decent, but they aren't.  Very very flat and not really what I would call virtual or 3D.

I'd say wait until Honor and Glory eventually release their game... this was kind of a waste of money.
When I bought this game I thought I would really like it but I actually hated i maybe played it once
I think they could have done better with submarine

My first encounter with Titanic VR was when Oculus first introduced Oculus Link to the Quest and it was the first title i purchased to try out. I was blown away, I even emailed them asking if there were any plans for a standalone version for the Quest but never got a response although back then i was clueless about requirements etc so looking back it probably came across as a ridiculous request. 
Imagine 5 years later seeing "Coming Soon " on the store. Straight on my Wishlist and i was ecstatic counting down the days until now when it is finally here.
So Titanic VR is early Access with a lot still to be added but even so an amazing experience i can highly recommend and looks fantastic on the Quest 3. I have always used the sandbox mode simply because i was intrigued to explore however this time i decided to actually play the game and surprisingly there is an awesome game there.
There are some glitches here and there but not enough to ruin the experience and no doubt they will be sorted plus as i mentioned there are more features still to be added.
Overall i can highly recommend this experience and the price is excellent for what you get as it is let alone the features still to be added. 
Thank you so much for bringing this to the Quest.
The cockpit is still way to dark in the "Short Tour" as its almost black ... UPDATE ... The sinking has now been added and to be honest looks far better than i expected so well done Devs on that. Obviously some texture sacrifices had to be made but not that noticeable unless you directly compare to the PC. I did think the Quest 3 was crisper and the lighting was better in certain areas also.
Great job and very happy
Really enjoying this  and hoping to see more of this type of media.

Originally I have this on the PC. Seeing it as a stand alone for quest 3 I was curious on how it was hold up to the PC version. Now I have seen it and it is not worth the money. Going to refund as soon as I get home.

14/04/2025:
I had this Titanic Simulator on my former PSVR and whilst I enjoyed that immensely, I eventually sold the platform and came to Meta. I had been plugging this simulator for well over a year and finally, it is here. Dreams do come true.

I purchased this on its release date and couldn’t wait to download and immerse myself into it. I actually happy cried because I loved the PSVR so much and so far, this is exactly the same as I once played.

I cannot thank the developers enough for bringing this to Meta. The graphics and sound are far much better in this Meta version compared to my former PSVR version.

If you love the Titanic and its history. You will totally enjoy this immersive exploration and being released on the day it foundered in 1912, has its special place in my heart. 

18/04/2025:
Been down to the Turkish Baths with the ROV and the detailing of the ceramic and glass tiles are amazing :)

11/06/2025:
Completed the game and done more of my own searching of certain areas.
Captain Smith’s bath is now shown in his cabin. Still full of water ;-)
Debris Field: Smoke Stacks East of Bow Section. Titanic’s Bell, 1st Officer William Murdoch’s Pistol and Duffle Bag located <70mtrs North of Bow Section. Cargo 1 Hold Cover South West of Bow Section.

14/06/2025:
((( SPECIAL LOCATION)))
Just came across this.
Travel 227mtrs SWS from Grand Staircase (approx 50mtrs SWS from Cargo Hatch #1) and you will find the Oceangate Submersible that was attempting a dive to the Titanic in 2023. Powerful addition to this VR Experience.

Just waiting for the final item to be added to this simulation.

05/08/2025:
Updated for “Experience”.
No simulation has brought tears to my eyes. This update has brought a truly remarkable addition to this simulator, of the final moments before Titanic foundered. The families, splitting of the males from their families. The rowing away from the ship. To the final part, when she broke up and sank below the surface of the North Atlantic. Such a powerful scene. Who dares not to shed a tear, for they are cold hearted. Nice job devs :)

Best Simulator and I totally recommend it :-)

Still no update ..still no full release ..feel like it's been abandoned ..like many early access games we pay on the promise a game or experience will be completed .. we trust & handover our hard-earned money 

A few weeks has now turned into a few months 

I understand the difficulties & time it can take but at the very least keep us updated 

I hope this gets finished I really do am so tired of being let down 

**Update we have the sinking experience ** cheers for reaching out Devs it looks great ...good job **

No update for the sinking of the titanic experience. Feels abandoned. Very disappointed. 

It was good experiencing the wreck but once main part is done, there is nothing to do. 
don't promise things you can't deliver.

Such a shame and I don't really understand why it can't be achievable considering what other games have accomplished on this hardware.

developer_response.body:
Hi Tim,

Thank you for your feedback.

We have just released the 'April 15th 1912 Lifeboat Experience' update.

Our apologies for the delayed release. Due to the technical challenges involved and the non-interactive nature of the experience we wanted to ensure the highest possible quality before releasing the update.

Thank you for supporting us during early access.

reviews.review_description:
***** UPDATED REVIEW AFTER APRIL EXPERIENCE ADDED *****



(Quick note - DID YOU FORGET TO PUT AN EXIT THE EXPERIENCE BUTTON! hope if anyone starts it they dont plan on exiting without closing the whole app!)

Disappointing...

Now I know not to compare the PCVR version to thw standalone version but we're not trying to bring over Half Life Alyx graphics here.

Yes, the main "experience" is all intact but the issues below are still a negative that outweighs any positives.

1. The audio is either broken or wasn't tested / just throw in - because upon the ship at the start all the characters' audio comes from directly in front of you, irrelevant to who is talking. When the girl is placed on the boat you look to the left to see her and her voice is then coming from your right ear (from the same place the whole conversation audio was playing from) instant immersion breaking.

The audio then when on the row boat all just plays directly in your ears no matter who is speaking, which takes away the experience even more as you have no idea who is speaking, at one point I sat there thinking "wait am I an actual character on the boat and I'm speaking here" 

I wasn't expecting a fully simulated ocean but to just have the whole sea/water pitch black (accept from if you look down to your left and you see what looks like static concrete and not anything that resembles water) ruins the experience even more.

Upon going back to the PCVR version just to compare, that didn't render a full ocean but just enough where the lights were hitting it to give you that feel of being in actual water. 

Also missing even just the slightest glow of the ship lights on the water as it sinks is massively noticeable from the original to this port.

In short yes I will still probably let people see it as it's easier than carrying around my pc to show someone the better version but it would be nice if a few minor tweaks were made to at least get the standalone experience to a better place.

- Fix the audio positioning on the ship and the rowboat.

- add some kind of water look to parts where light is catching it, even just some white wiggling lines would look better than pure black abyss.

- The final part of the ship after it has split and is sinking is too brightly lit (the original was much darker which gives it a more eerie feeling seeing the ship in darkness with no power disappearing into the ocean)

This version has this light wood colour which makes the boat look like it's incorrectly lit in the scene.


I hope these things can be added/improved (ESPECIALLY THE AUDIO AS THAT IS JUST BROKEN for an experience that is heavily relying on being in the moment with these characters)

-------------------------------------------------------


(Original review below after purchase)

I purchased this game (I say game lightly)

I own it on pc and solely purchased the Quest version so i can easily let people experience the best part of this app, the Titanic sinking experience. On PC this was the most uncomfortable experience ever (as in seeing what happened in 1st person and watching the ship sink was something that cant be explained, hence me buying the question version to allow people to see it easily)

But that part says COMING SOON!!! so what have i paid for??? The VERY OUTDATED "game" part, snap turn, if you activate smooth turn on the drone it takes about 2 hours to turn around. 

How is this out for the price it is and 1 part of the app isnt even in the game yet!! This should of came out at 2.99!!!

I have no idea at all how 1 of the reviews on here says graphic update on the quest and looks stunning! It looks pretty much the same as the pc version from however many years ago it released. Underwater looks nice with loads of stuttering, and in the base where you do the washing the items etc looks like the graphics are set to potato mode. Zero graphic upgrade so do not believe anyone who says others.

This is a straight port and is missing the best part of the whole experience!

But its "coming soon" apparently!!?? Just not when the game is released to everyone! 

DO NOT PURCHASE.

*in reponse to the devs trying to justify it with "its early access the rest comes later" then maybe release it at half price then.

Updated review after game update ----

Actually got excited to see it had Updated so jumped straight in to finally be able to show people the titanic crash experience! 

I then find out the update was the titanic tour part and STILL we have to wait for the 1 part everyone Actually wants.

So now you can either play the story part in the sub going round the titanic OR let ir drive you around the titanic... such a great variety.

If i could id lower the review to 0 stars.

This is as close as I will ever get to seeing the titanic. This is an awesome experience. Great job to all the developers for working on this for quest users. Thank you for all the hard work put into your game. Patiently waiting on update to make the experience better. P.S. could you add a play station controller on the ocean floor. Thanks! 😉 YES!!! Finally you get to experience the sinking from a lifeboat.

reviews.review_title: W game im a titanic fan
reviews.review_description: Wwwwww
I took 2h to download so I didn’t get to play I till the next day

They did release the update. The lighting could use to be tweaked because everything just looks grey due to the LCD panel not playing well with pitch black. All in all the content is okay. The graphics could be better. I'd like the ability to rotate the camera to see what's going on around me without having to physically move my body.
Seen worse, but seen much better.

Premium platinum Dom Perignon champagne grade app. Intense. Surpasses all expectations.

*Whew! An unforgettable historical adventure on an immense scale.
A must own now the app has been updated. Amazing experience.

Third quest game in a row that I’ve purchased that’s been a glitchfest. What’s going lately with quest games? Stuck early on cleaning the recovered suitcase that won’t clean. Plus! Isn’t this an 8 year old game? So why arent all the features available on release (Experience mode etc).

developer_response.body:
The issue with the case has now been patched. To pass this section you only need to clean the journal, open it and read the receipt to progress to the next level. This is an early access game so other parts will be released in the next few weeks. The orginal game is 8 years old but this had to be compleatly rebuilt to support the Quest hardware. We focused on the ship exploration game part for the anniversary.

Update: Hi Pitbully, Thank you for your feedback. We have just released the 'April 15th 1912 Lifeboat Experience' update.

Our apologies for the delayed release. Due to the technical challenges involved and the non-interactive nature of the experience we wanted to ensure the highest possible quality before releasing the update.

All origional content from the PC version, as well as additional Quest 3 only content included in our 'Oceangate Memorial & Titanic Wreck Site Tour' is now released. Thank you for supporting us during early access.

reviews.review_description:
I'm a true enthusiast of Titanic experiences and everything related to it — and I wasn't disappointed.

I had already purchased it back when it was first released on the PlayStation Store for PSVR, and while the graphics were quite limited at the time, this Quest 3 version is absolutely stunning, with a clear graphical improvement.

I highly recommend it, especially for the exploration mode, which is truly captivating.

My only criticism is that inside the Titanic’s bow, there are some doors you can enter, but the textures often clip all over the place and display random visual glitches.

Basically, when you're in a narrow room and get too close to a wall, the textures disappear, which can be quite disorienting at times.

Fix this, and the experience would be perfect.

I need to add something to a comment saying I'm lying about the graphics.

This guy says the PC version has the same graphics as on Quest 3.

But we’re talking about PC here — PC is the top tier — and that’s exactly why I said the graphics are good on Quest 3. Because, I repeat, I didn’t play on PC but on PSVR on the PlayStation 4.

So in the end, the guy is just embarrassing himself — the PC version is the best-looking one, and he's saying it's identical to the Quest 3.
I bought this game on Easter and there hasn’t been an update since then.￼

developer_response.body:
Hi Jam,

Thank you for your feedback.

We have just released the 'April 15th 1912 Lifeboat Experience' update.

Our apologies for the delayed release. Due to the technical challenges involved and the non-interactive nature of the experience we wanted to ensure the highest possible quality before releasing the update.

Thank you for supporting us during early access.

reviews.review_title: This is the stuff that makes VR magical
reviews.review_description: This experience isn't without its flaws and issues, but seeing as it is in Early Access at the moment I am sure those will get ironed out. What is here though, is much like on the PC version, an absolutely magical experience. Going down to the actual wreck isn't something most of us can do, or maybe even would want to do considering the risk, but in VR it's absolutely incredible. Sitting in a tiny sub at the ocean floor with the ship in front of you really gives you a sense of scale that's impossible to experience any other way. Also look forward to when the surface experience gets added as well. This is the stuff that VR was made for!

I’ve been aching to do the tour of the wreck but the moment I go for it , it crashes and says low on memory.
I have 512 gb version with over 200 gb free so how can I it be low.
No problem with any other games or experiences.

developer_response.body:
Hi Clockworktash,

Thank you for reporting your crash due to low memory.

This has been reported across numerous titles on the Meta Quest store and is caused by specific versions of the Meta Quest 3 Device Software.

You can resolve this by updating your Meta Quest 3 Device to the latest version, currently v78.1030, and restarting the device.

We have also included significant memory reductions in the 'Dive' sections of the app in our 'April 15th 1912 Lifeboat Experience' update we released today to help buffer against any future increased memory requirements made by the device software.

Thank you for supporting us during early access.

reviews.review_title: Thanks for bringing this to Quest!
reviews.review_description:
Just wow! I backed this experience on Kickstarter in 2017 when it was originally announced for PCVR/Steam release, and can say that the devs have made a fantastic job of bringing this to stand alone Meta Quest! The wreck is so real that I plan to use real deck plans of Titanic and use that to navigate around the ship.

I have only been using the sandbox mode so far but it easily makes the whole experience worth it. There’s some minor improvements I want to suggest (listed below) but overall this is truly a 5 star app on the Meta Store. And remember that this is released in Early Access just so it was possible to hit the 113 year anniversary, and more features will be released as free updates during the weeks/months to come.

Don’t hesitate if you’re a fan of exploring, this is a true gem! Watch some Titanic documentaries to get into the mood, and familiarize yourself with the ship/wreck and you will for sure enjoy the experience a lot more.

Improvement suggestions:
- Even though the sub interior light is pretty much perfect in my opinion, then introduce brightness adjustment for the interior light in the sub. Some would like to have it dimmer and some want it brighter.
- Setting to adjust audio/music. I for one would like to have the option to turn off/lower the music or change score.
- ROV movement feels a bit unrealistic, i.e. when engaging/releasing the forward/backward control I would expect the ROV to accelerate/drift slightly to make it feel more realistic. Make this ”accelerate/drift at stop” optional in settings for those that would find it uncomfortable. Also, make it possible to move the ROV slower as it moves a bit too fast even at its slowest speed. Especially for those fine movement scenarios.
- Fix minor garphic glitches like items popping up.
- Adding shadows to be cast inside the wreck when exploring with the ROV.
- Maybe too much to ask, but it would be a great addition to be allowed to walk around freely on the promenade of Titanic (only the exterior) during the time of hitting the iceberg.

On a final note. As a recreational scuba diver that have been diving in wrecks then this really does give you the sensation of being underwater. Strongly recommended!

reviews.review_title: Early access - not quite there yet
reviews.review_description:
I'm afraid it still needs work, as of course it's an early access. I own the pc version on Steam VR, and I can make a couple of comparisons, after a handful of minutes:
1- The graphics are way too pixelated and flat. My version on Steam VR through Virtual Desktop looks way better than this one, so far. I would also work on the bump maps in the shaders, if possible, to give a bit more depth to the graphics. 
2- It's way too dark. I understand the environment is at the bottom of the ocean, but the Steam VR version is better balanced than this one. Here I can barely see, even inside the rover, and it doesn't feel correct. I would work on the lighting, maybe it needs a better HDRI on top of the local lights? 
I'm sure it'll improve with time, it's probably worth buying it and wait for further developments, but for me that I already have it on Steam, I'm afraid it's not worth it, yet. I'll give it some time to see if there are major improvements within the return window policy, otherwise I'll place a refund. I hope these points can be helpful. On Steam VR this experience is remarkable, especially if you're into the Titanic story as much as I do, and I appreciate the effort to bring it to the Quest 3. Keep up the good work :)
Best regards,
Alessandro

reviews.review_title: Absolutely love this. Can’t wait for upd
reviews.review_description: This is a great vr experience and in my book what vr is for. Only spent an hour on Q3 so far but I love it. It does have its glitches every so often but running it through QGO it’s so much better and graphics are a lot crisper than before. Can’t wait to see what comes next. As a 48 yr old titanic enthusiast this was a must for me so 100% satisfied with the purchase. Good job developers, I love it and so does my 11 yr old.

I have been eagerly waiting for this on the Quest for years and so far have not been disappointed.
The main part of the game when you go down in a submersible then use the RV to get inside the ship is a thing of dreams for me, the submersible looks amazing both outside and especially inside, the controls for both the sub and RV are both really responsive.
I’m also enjoying the lab parts where you clean objects.
It really just shows how far mobile untethered VR has come where they can run this and make it look so amazing.
I can wait for the other stuff to come later, too me that just shows the developers want to give us the best possible version of their product and i for one, trust them to deliver as they have so far.
Kudos to everyone involved in making this, a lot of love has been poured into and i am very thankful for this experience.
Paid for what I believed to be an immersive Vr experience for the ship Titanic. Instead, app crashes every attempt to launch a mission. Refund should be offered until fixed. Memory related issue.

Hi EdgyPufferfish,

reviews.review_description: I love titanic. And bought this as soon as it came out. But the only thing I don’t like is the submarine controls are kinda of bugged and hard to control. But besides that it’s a great game and cannot wait for the upcoming updates.

Happy to replaying this game again now on Quest 3, from what I noticed, in much better definition than on PSVR 1, in 2018.
I know the developers redesigned this version for Quest 3 from scratch and did it very well. 
It's great to play this educational game again. :)
It won't play on my quest 3. It says low memory. How do I get a refund?

reviews.review_description:
I love  this game I've been waiting for a game like this for years
The sub inside is ok and fine to control it ( weather it could have been made to look a little more relistic inside the sub?
And taken a little longer before you get to the ship just to build that tension,But otherwise I love it .
 when when you look out of the observation window ( porthole)
As it comes in to veiw its just like all the real footage you've seen.
You can take the sub to a part of the ship where you can enter then you 
Use Googles inside the sub to take the remote camera into the ship itself.
Beautifully Spooky to see how the ship must have looked in its day.
Recently added is a tour where you sit back and taken on a guided tour which is done Beautifully. 
And before I finish I forgot to say you can play a game where you collect artifacts and retrieve them.
I love this informative app/ game?
Beautiful.
5 stars all the way.

reviews.review_title: Very happy this is out now!
reviews.review_description:
I used to play this on my old Oculus Rift and really enjoyed it.  I was gutted when I switched to Meta Quest 3 and I couldn’t play this any more.  It’s very good and personally, I love it.  There have been a few changes and I’m sure there are some extra places in the wreck to explore.  The sound effects also seem a little spookier, but it all adds to the atmosphere.  I do agree with another reviewer that it does seem a little dark in places, but on the whole I enjoy it and it’s well worth it for anyone who is interested in the Titanic story.  
While I’m being entertained and experiencing it all from the safety of my living room, I think of the poor people that night who suffered such unimaginable fear.

reviews.review_title: Game not ready and no experience yet
reviews.review_description:
I was looking forward to seeing the experience but unfortunately was not available yet. 

Granted it is in early access so I thought I would give the main game a go. Started off well with good (ish) visuals for quest but after 10 mins or so I was unable to progress further with washing the briefcase. 

Hopefully this will be patched soon + the release of the experience but for now I don't think this should be purchased until these bugs are fixed first

developer_response.body:
Hi patch for briefcase is now live. To pass this section you only need to clean the journal which is in the case, open it and read the receipt to progress to the next level. This is an early access game so other parts will be released in the next few weeks.

Update: Hi Bownzy, Thank you for your feedback. We have just released the 'April 15th 1912 Lifeboat Experience' update. Our apologies for the delayed release. Due to the technical challenges involved and the non-interactive nature of the experience we wanted to ensure the highest possible quality before releasing the update. All origional content from the PC version, as well as additional Quest 3 only content included in our 'Oceangate Memorial & Titanic Wreck Site Tour' is now released. Thank you for supporting us during early access.

reviews.review_title: It's good if a bit slow
reviews.review_description:
Needs hands.
Objects such as case awkward to move and it's hard to get in machine in lab to dry.
Black screen for 5 secs just before getting to titanic when going to get wallet /docs near safe(secondmission)..came back OK but  kinda froze quest up..thought it had totally crashed headset..hasn't happened again.
Get the 2 experiences patched in asap..kinda disappointing not seeing ship go down before starting actual game.
Smooth turning too slow in rover.
Don't really like teleport system to get to chairs in sub..please fix that to full movement.
Sort it out and 5 stars..still happy I bought it.

developer_response.body:
Hi Monz,

We have just released the 'April 15th 1912 Lifeboat Experience' update. Our apologies for the delayed release. Due to the technical challenges involved and the non-interactive nature of the experience we wanted to ensure the highest possible quality before releasing the update.

Thank you for your feedback on the dive and lab missions. As we have been completely focused on releasing the experience as soon as possible we have haven't yet addressed any of your other feedback, but will notify you when we do.

Thank you for supporting us during early access.

reviews.review_description: Thank you for letting us share this experience with you. Everyone who made it possible we look forward to more of your work and creations.

Awesome game keep up the good I know more stuff is coming soon update game is,a,black screen can't do anything been like that for days now I'm kind of mad about it but. I know you guys will fix it in due time.

developer_response.body: HI Quirky. Do you still have this issue? We tested the version you are on and it was fine on our Quest 3 and 3S. Did you install the latest update we pushed out on Thursday?

reviews.review_description: Brilliant game very relaxing for 68 year old a few glitches but that's expected just getting into meta quest 3s as well
Loving this so far. So haunting.
